description_long: This bungalow features a low, elongated profile with a gently sloped, intersecting roof structure in a light tan color. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral tone, likely painted wood or stucco, and the building sits on a green lawn base. The front facade includes a covered porch area with steps leading up, and large windows that allow ample natural light into the living spaces. The side and rear elevations show a mix of window sizes, with some grouped together and others spaced apart, contributing to an asymmetrical window arrangement. A simple fence is visible on one side, suggesting a boundary or garden area, and the overall design is functional and modest, typical of mid-century suburban bungalows.
description: A single-story, light-colored bungalow with a tan intersecting roof, covered porch, and asymmetrical window arrangement, set on a green lawn with a side fence.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, entry steps, and large windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the building, the porch steps, and the adjacent fence.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying smaller windows and the intersecting roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with grouped windows and a clear view of the fence.
